root_cause: loadInventory() is called as a floating promise — no await, no returned cleanup function. The useEffect has no way to cancel the in-flight fetch when the component unmounts or warehouseId changes. When the fetch completes after unmount, setInventory fires on an unmounted component.
fix: Use an AbortController: create one at the top of useEffect, pass its signal to fetch, and return a cleanup function that calls controller.abort(). Catch AbortError separately to avoid setting error state on intentional cancellation.
